cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking which of the following statements is wrong according to the news article provided. First, I need to go through each option and compare it with the details in the text. Option A says: "will give up the space program because of the accident." The article mentions that President Bush said, "America's space program will go on," which directly contradicts option A. So A is likely the wrong statement. Option B states: "NASA hasn't found the cause of the Columbia disaster." The text says, "NASA... said that although there had been some data failures it was too early to nail down a precise cause." This aligns with B, so B is correct according to the news. Option C: "Before the Columbia disaster, no shuttles had exploded in the course of landing." The article states, "In 42 years of US' human space flight, there had never been an accident in the descent to Earth or landing. Challenger exploded just after take-off."
